content.problemStatement:
There are nnn friends. The ithi^{\text{th}}ith friend has AiA_iAi​ coins. You are also given an integer RRR.

Define a transaction as the transfer of one coin from one friend to another. They can do as many transactions as they want under the constraint that:


For any ordered pair of friends iii and jjj, the number of transactions from iii to jjj doesn't exceed RRR.
What is the maximum possible number of coins the friend with the minimum coins can have in the end?

Please note that the number of coins held by a person can't go negative at any time in the process of transactions.


Input Format

First line will contain nnn and RRR.
Then the second line contains nnn integers separated by a space, A1,A2,…,AnA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_nA1​,A2​,…,An​.


Output Format
Print the maximum possible number of coins the friend with the minimum coins can have in the end.
